sla_config.name: QualiTrace_AI_SLAConfig
sla_config.description: SLA timer configuration for all human task types in QualiTrace AI. All time values are in hours. Warning notification fires at 75% of elapsed time. SLA breach escalates to quality_lead and is written to the audit trail. All four risk_level values are defined separately — do not collapse any of them.

global_sla_rules.time_unit_note: All duration values in this file are expressed in hours. No minutes, no mixed units. Convert to milliseconds at runtime if the Maestro timer engine requires it.
global_sla_rules.warning_threshold_note: Warning notification fires when (elapsed_time / sla_limit) >= 0.75. This is exactly 75% — not 50%, not 80%.
global_sla_rules.breach_audit_note: Every SLA breach must be written to the case audit trail with event type SLA_BREACH before any escalation action is taken. The audit entry must record: case_id, task_type, risk_level, sla_limit_hours, elapsed_hours, escalated_to.

risk_level_human_review_sla.description: SLA time limits for human_review tasks, keyed by risk_level. All four risk_level values are defined independently. Do not merge high and critical — they have different time limits and different routing behaviour upstream.
risk_level_human_review_sla.note: These values apply to task_type values: risk_review and critical_escalation when those tasks are triggered by the corresponding risk_level. See task_sla_rules below for the authoritative per-task mapping.

rules.description: Critical cases must receive a human decision within 4 hours of task creation. Warning fires at 3 hours (75% of 4). This is the tightest SLA in the system — no exceptions.
High risk cases must receive a human decision within 24 hours. Warning fires at 18 hours (75% of 24).
Medium risk cases must receive a human decision within 72 hours. Warning fires at 54 hours (75% of 72).
Low risk cases must receive a human decision within 168 hours (7 days). Warning fires at 126 hours (75% of 168).

task_sla_rules.description: Authoritative SLA configuration per task_type. Each entry maps directly to a template in HumanTaskTemplates.json. Where a task inherits its SLA from risk_level, the lookup key is risk_level_human_review_sla above. Where a task has a fixed SLA, it is stated directly here.

missing_info.description: Missing info tasks inherit the SLA of the paused case's risk_level. A critical case paused for missing batch_number still has a 4-hour window to resolve. Timer starts when the task is created, not when the case was originally submitted.
missing_info.timer_pause_note: The SLA timer does NOT pause when the case pauses for missing_info. The clock keeps running from the moment the task is created.

risk_review.description: Risk review tasks are triggered by confidence_score < 0.6. SLA is set by the case's risk_level at the time of task creation.

capa_approval.description: CAPA approval tasks carry a fixed 48-hour SLA regardless of the case's risk_level. Warning fires at 36 hours (75% of 48). This fixed limit applies whether the underlying case is critical or low risk.
capa_approval.risk_level_override_note: risk_level does NOT change the CAPA approval SLA. It is always 48 hours.

critical_escalation.description: Critical escalation tasks always carry a 4-hour SLA. This task is only created when risk_level == 'critical', so the fixed value matches the critical row in risk_level_human_review_sla. Stated explicitly here for clarity and so Maestro does not need a lookup — it can read the value directly.

breach_secondary_action.description: On breach, NotificationSender.xaml fires immediately in addition to the standard audit entry and escalation notification.

final_closure.description: Final closure tasks inherit the SLA of the case's risk_level. A critical case awaiting final sign-off still has a 4-hour window. Timer starts when the task is created at the conclusion of Stage 6 EffectivenessReview.

warning_notification_spec.description: Specification for the warning notification that fires at 75% of SLA elapsed.

notification_payload.message: SLA warning: this task has used 75% of its allocated time. Action required before breach.

warning_notification_spec.audit_entry_note: Warning notifications do not require an audit trail entry. Only breach events write to the audit trail.

breach_spec.description: Specification for actions taken when an SLA is breached (elapsed_time >= sla_limit_hours).

step_1.note: Audit entry must be written BEFORE escalation notification is sent. This is step 1 — it cannot be skipped.

notification_payload.message: SLA BREACH: this task has exceeded its SLA limit. Immediate attention required.

breach_does_not: remove the task from the quality_lead queue

sla_summary_table.description: Quick reference. All values in hours. Warning always at 75% of limit.

field_references.note: All field names are exact snake_case from CaseDefinition.json. No camelCase anywhere in this file.
